repository.name: linx
repository.description: Real-time Academic Collaboration Platform

commit.message:
feat: build complete Linx MERN academic collaboration platform

- Express/Node.js backend with JWT auth, rate limiting, CORS
- MongoDB models: User, Project, Job, Activity, Message
- Full CRUD controllers for auth, users, projects, jobs, activities
- Socket.io real-time: activity feed, project rooms, presence tracking
- Academic email verification (.edu, .ac.uk, .edu.in, etc.)
- GitHub integration for skill extraction
- React 18 + Vite frontend with Tailwind CSS
- Glassmorphism UI with Framer Motion animations
- Zustand auth store with localStorage persistence
- Landing page with hero, stats, features, testimonials
- Dashboard (bento grid), Projects, Jobs, Alumni, Profile pages
- Reusable components: Button, Card, Navbar, Footer, modals
- useAuth, useSocket, useGitHub custom hooks
- Axios API service with auth interceptors
- Socket.io client service
- SETUP.md and updated README.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Support dashboard resources auto-downloading (issue #985) (#1062)

* Initial plan

* feat: add dashboard auto-download support (external-ui-url config)

Co-authored-by: ibigbug <543405+ibigbug@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: IPv6 literals fail in direct outbound when dns.ipv6 is disabled (#1063)

* fix(tun): use async sleep to wait for TUN interface visibility before route setup (#1060)

* Initial plan

* fix(tun): wait for tun visibility using async sleep before adding routes

Fixes #1032 - intermittent TUN startup crash "tun interface not found".

After build_async() creates a new TUN device, the interface may not be
immediately visible via NetworkInterface::show(). This caused maybe_add_routes()
to call get_interface_by_name() -> None -> .expect() -> panic (SIGABRT).

Changes:
- Add Clone derive to TunConfig (config/internal/config.rs)
- Replace .expect("tun interface not found") with proper error return in
  maybe_add_routes (routes/mod.rs)
- Make new_internal an async associated function taking &TunConfig; add
  retry loop (up to 40x50ms = 2s) using tokio::time::sleep (not
  std::thread::sleep which blocks Tokio runtime) to wait for TUN
  interface visibility before calling maybe_add_routes (runner.rs)
- Update run_async to clone cfg and await new_internal inside tokio::spawn

Co-authored-by: ibigbug <543405+ibigbug@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(tun): address review comments - fix poll loop and surface NetworkInterface errors

- Restructure TUN visibility poll loop to avoid sleeping after the
  last attempt, eliminating false-negative window where interface
  becomes visible during the final sleep but still errors
- Capture NetworkInterface::show() errors and include the last error
  message in the Error::Operation return for better diagnostics
- Fix fmt/clippy issues in runner.rs

Co-authored-by: ibigbug <543405+ibigbug@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(tun): fix nightly fmt - adjust line break in format string

Co-authored-by: ibigbug <543405+ibigbug@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: ibigbug <543405+ibigbug@users.noreply.github.com>

* feature: support vless reality (#1057)

* feat: support inbound provider (#1066)

* feat: allow proxy groups/providers as outbounds for DNS and geo-data downloads (#1061)

* Initial plan

* style: fix cargo fmt formatting in dashboard module

* feat: use proxy-aware HTTP client for dashboard download

Co-authored-by: ibigbug <543405+ibigbug@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(skills): add adr skill with best practices

- Remove broken \ template variables — Claude extracts all
  context from conversation
- Replace flat requirements list with a 4-step process: extract context,
  check existing ADRs for sequential numbering, draft inline for review,
  then save
- Add writing guidelines with WHY reasoning (context vs problem, honest
  trade-offs, rejection rationale for alternatives)
- Remove mandatory coded bullet points (POS-001, NEG-001 etc.) — replaced
  with clean bullets; template sections marked optional
- Add status lifecycle table: Proposed → Accepted → Superseded with
  semantics for each state
- Define output format: file path printed after save with one-line summary
- Rewrite description with diverse trigger phrases and proactive use guidance
- README: add adr skill entry with feature list, update badge count to 15,
  add to repo structure

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(grpc): add unit and integration tests for gRPC server and v6 handler

- Add tests/ut/grpc/server/server_test.go: 10 tests (miniredis + bufconn)
- Add tests/ut/domain/v6/handler_test.go: 11 tests (miniredis + real TCP)
- Add tests/it/v6_test.go: 10 IT tests using router.New() and full pipeline
- Refactor grpc/server: replace test-only NewArticleServiceServer() with NewServer()
- Refactor redis: remove test-only InitWithAddr(); tests use t.Setenv + Init()
- Make v6.GRPCDialFunc private; inject gRPC address via GRPC_ADDR env var in tests
- Add gRPC client unary interceptor for request/response structured logging
- Add .dockerignore to prevent .env from being copied into Docker build context
- Fix docker-compose: use env_file to pass .env into container (restore DB/Redis config)
- Update README with Docker setup notes (set DB_HOST=mysql, REDIS_HOST=redis in .env)
- Update docs/grpc.md: use -proto flag in grpcurl commands (no reflection required)
- Add GRPC_ADDR to .env.example

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Stripe integration: serverless via Payment Links + DNFT card checkout (GitHub Pages / IPFS compatible) (#157)

* Initial plan

* feat: integrate Stripe credit/debit card subscriptions (#41)

- Add server.js: Express backend with Stripe Checkout, webhook, Customer Portal, and session endpoints
- Add js/stripe-config.js: browser-safe config for publishable key + Price IDs
- Update js/subscription.js: replace TODO stub with real /api/stripe/create-checkout-session call; add openStripePortal() export
- Update js/app.js: handle ?stripe=success/?stripe=cancel return URLs; wire About-page "Pay with Card" button (respects Monthly/Annual toggle); wire Customer Portal footer link
- Update index.html: add Stripe.js SDK script; load stripe-config.js; activate Stripe button (remove Coming Soon)
- Update css/styles.css: add .sub-toast notification styles; add .stripe-btn:hover state
- Update package.json: add express + stripe deps; add npm start script
- Update .env.example: add STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, STRIPE_MONTHLY_PRICE_ID, STRIPE_ANNUAL_PRICE_ID
- Add docs/STRIPE_SETUP.md: full walkthrough (keys, products, webhook CLI, portal, test cards)

Security: sk_* and whsec_* keys are only read from process.env in server.js — never in frontend code

Co-authored-by: TheJollyLaMa <75486638+TheJollyLaMa@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/TheJollyLaMa/BigNuten_Vanilla/sessions/ce27fb8b-814c-48dd-bdad-aed9f3d7735a

* fix: switch Stripe to Payment Links — serverless, works on GitHub Pages & IPFS

The backend Checkout Session approach required server.js to run, which is
incompatible with static hosting (GitHub Pages, IPFS). Replace with Stripe
Payment Links — pre-created checkout URLs from the Stripe Dashboard, opened
directly by the browser (same pattern as PayPal buttons).

- js/stripe-config.js: replace publishable key + Price IDs with Payment Link
  URLs (STRIPE_MONTHLY_PAYMENT_LINK, STRIPE_ANNUAL_PAYMENT_LINK, STRIPE_PORTAL_URL)
- js/subscription.js: rewrite initStripeSubscription() to window.location.href;
  rewrite openStripePortal() to window.open(portalUrl); add STRIPE_PLACEHOLDER_SENTINEL
- js/app.js: remove /api/stripe/session backend fetch; add _getStripePaymentLink()
  + _stripeNotConfigured() helpers; use helpers in sub-modal and About-page buttons
- index.html: remove Stripe.js SDK script; fix stripe-config.js comment
- server.js: add prominent 'DO YOU NEED THIS? NO for GitHub Pages/IPFS' header
- docs/STRIPE_SETUP.md: rewrite as 3-step Payment Links guide (no server)
- .env.example: clarify all Stripe vars are optional (server.js only)

Co-authored-by: TheJollyLaMa <75486638+TheJollyLaMa@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/TheJollyLaMa/BigNuten_Vanilla/sessions/8ab62b07-4aaa-4ef5-92dc-7c7b1536d182

* chore: remove server.js and server deps left over from first Stripe attempt

The first Stripe commit added a Node.js Express backend (server.js) and the
express/stripe npm dependencies. Now that we use Stripe Payment Links (no
server required), none of that is needed.

- Delete server.js entirely
- Revert package.json to pre-Stripe state (remove express, stripe deps and npm start script)
- Simplify .env.example Stripe section: no server means no secret keys to document

Co-authored-by: TheJollyLaMa <75486638+TheJollyLaMa@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/TheJollyLaMa/BigNuten_Vanilla/sessions/b411666e-ed67-4017-bc3a-f0a7af7d4591

* Update Stripe payment and portal links

* style: unify PayPal/Stripe/Crypto payment buttons to match crypto button aesthetic

- PayPal button: replace the PayPal GIF (btn_subscribeCC_LG.gif) with plain "PayPal"
  text label — no credit card imagery, full unified style
- Stripe button: keep "💳 Pay with Card" label; add VISA and MC card badges
- All three buttons now share the same cyan glow background + border (previously
  only the Crypto button had this look)
- Add .card-badges / .card-badge-visa / .card-badge-mc styles for the card badges

commit.message:
Feat/emacs pkg (#4)

* feat: add summary DTOs and QuerySummaries for lightweight log listing

- Create internal/management/dto.go with LogSummary, LogListResponse,
  LogDetailResponse, LogDetailEntry, StreamViewResponse, BodyResponse,
  and APIError types
- Add database.LogSummary struct to models.go for DB scan targets
- Add LogReader.QuerySummaries() method selecting only summary columns
  (no bodies/headers) with computed body-length fields
- Update apiLogsHandler to use QuerySummaries and return LogListResponse
  envelope with pagination metadata (limit, offset, has_more)
- Update apiLogByIDHandler to return LogDetailResponse with decoded
  headers and no body fields
- Add writeAPIError, decodeHeaders, derefStr helpers
- Add ManagementConfig.PreviewBytes (default 65536) with validation
- Update example-config.yaml with preview_bytes setting
- Remove old logDetailResponse/streamViewResponse types from api.go

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add FTS5 full-text search indexing for openai_logs

- Add ftsSchema constant with FTS5 virtual table and sync triggers
- Add migrateFTS() with automatic rebuild for existing data
- Update buildWhere() to use FTS5 MATCH instead of LIKE
- Add FTS5 search test with distinct body content
- Enable sqlite_fts5 build tag in build script and Dockerfile

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Wire query parser into list API and add body retrieval endpoint

- Add query DSL support to apiLogsHandler via the 'query' parameter,
  using internal/query.Parse and query.ToSQL. Old individual params
  (status, path, since, until, q) remain as backward-compatible fallback.
- Add QuerySummariesRaw and CountRaw methods to LogReader that accept
  extra WHERE clause and args for the parsed query DSL.
- Add GET /api/logs/{id}/body endpoint with part (req/resp), mode
  (raw/assembled), and full parameters. Uses streamview.Build for
  assembled mode.
- Refactor apiLogByIDHandler into apiLogSubHandler that dispatches
  between detail and body sub-routes.
- Add PreviewBytes field to ServerDeps, wired from config.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update web UI for new management API endpoint split

- Replace three separate filter inputs (status, path, search) with a
  unified query DSL input that sends a 'query' parameter
- Update detail modal to use new API shape: headers are now parsed
  objects (req_headers/resp_headers) instead of JSON strings
- Add on-demand body loading via GET /api/logs/{id}/body endpoint
  with Load Request/Response Body buttons and Load Full for truncated
- Stream view assembled toggle now fetches from body endpoint
- Show included_bytes / total_bytes info from body responses
- Update index.html to match new single query filter input

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add comprehensive tests for body endpoint, query DSL, and summary shape

- Body endpoint tests (10): raw req/resp, assembled SSE, invalid
  combination, preview truncation, full mode, not found, empty body,
  default params, invalid part
- Query DSL wiring tests (2): filter by status code, parse error
- Summary list shape test (1): verify has_request_body/has_response_body
  present and body/header fields absent

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: emacs pkg: memoryelaine (and adptions)

  Implementation Complete — All 21 Tasks Done

  Phase 1: Go Server (11 tasks)

   - API redesign: Summary/detail/body endpoint split — list returns lightweight summaries, detail returns metadata+headers (no bodies), body endpoint serves content on demand
   - FTS5 indexing: Full-text search on request/response bodies with sync triggers and auto-migration
   - Query DSL parser: status:200 method:POST path:/chat "exact phrase" with parameterized SQL (no injection)
   - Web UI + TUI: Updated to use new API (query DSL input, on-demand body loading)
   - 28 server tests (14 new), updated docs and design spec

  Phase 2: Emacs Package (10 tasks)

  8 modules in /work/emacs/:

   - memoryelaine.el — entry point, defcustoms, autoloads
   - memoryelaine-auth.el — auth-source → explicit → interactive prompt
   - memoryelaine-http.el — async curl with generation counters, stale response suppression
   - memoryelaine-state.el — global search + buffer-local detail state
   - memoryelaine-search.el — tabulated-list-mode with query DSL, pagination, recording toggle, live search
   - memoryelaine-show.el — detail buffer with on-demand body loading, stream view toggle
   - memoryelaine-log.el — timestamped diagnostic buffer
   - memoryelaine-test.el — 25 ERT tests, all passing

  Test Results

   - 143 Go tests passing | 25 Emacs ERT tests passing

* Emacs build script, code review, and second opinion

* docs: fix mismatches between spec/README and Go implementation

- Fix DSL flag names: is:truncated -> is:req-truncated/is:resp-truncated,
  has:req-body/has:resp-body -> has:req/has:resp (matches parser.go)
- Fix assembled mode description: returns available:false with reason,
  does not fall back to raw (matches api.go)
- Add limit, offset, has_more to /api/logs response example (matches dto.go)
- Add Emacs client to list of consumer surfaces

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Opus-4.6: Address review from Gemini 3.1 Pro and gpt-5.4

* Add Gemini 3.1 Pro's patch review.

* Add Qwen's review

* gpt-5.4 final review

* Update spec, reorder design docs

* Also run emacs tests in CI

* Fix linting

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(tool-guard): add default git-safety and destructive-ops presets

Introduces skills/tool-guard/defaults/ with two composable policy presets:
- git-safety.json: deny force-push/reset-hard/clean-f; warn bare-push/branch-D/checkout/restore
- destructive-ops.json: deny rm -rf variants; warn mv with ../abs-path + PowerShell equivalents

Adds Step 0 to SKILL.md offering these presets before custom input collection.
Expands description with trigger phrases for git guardrail and destructive-delete requests.

Reviewer findings addressed:
- git push --force reason no longer recommends --force-with-lease (also caught by pattern)
- mv destination-position limitation documented in preset _description

commit.message:
fix(tool-guard): fix emoji corruption in generated PowerShell hooks

ConvertTo-Json on Windows does not escape non-ASCII to \\uXXXX by
default, causing the warning emoji (U+26A0 U+FE0F) to render as
'??' in many console environments.

Changes applied to copilot-cli.md and claude-code.md PS1 templates:
- Add [Console]::OutputEncoding = UTF8 / \System.Text.UTF8Encoding+UTF8EncodingSealed = UTF8 header
- Replace literal emoji strings with \⚠\️ var
- Add -EscapeHandling EscapeNonAscii to ConvertTo-Json (PS 7.1+)

Also added a generation rule to SKILL.md and a note to README.md so
future consumers know why char codes are used and don't 'fix' it back.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: validate trees on backup restore and legacy migration

Add validateTree() to backup-manager restoreFullReplace/restoreMerge
and chart-store legacy localStorage migration. Invalid trees are
skipped (backup) or replaced with defaults (migration) instead of
silently corrupting IndexedDB.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: arbol
repository.description: Browser-based org chart editor with real-time D3 visualization

commit.message:
fix: validate inputs before snapshot in OrgStore mutations

Reorder addChild, removeNode, updateNode, and fromJSON to validate
inputs before calling snapshot(). Failed mutations no longer leave
orphan entries in the undo stack.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: improve theme preset title colors for WCAG AA contrast

Change titleColor from #64748b (4.7:1) to #475569 (7.5:1) in 7 theme
presets. Add WCAG AA contrast ratio regression tests for all presets.
All presets now comfortably exceed WCAG AA 4.5:1 minimum contrast ratio.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor(tests): restructure ut/ to mirror app/ package layout

- Move flat tests/ut/*.go into subdirectories matching app/ structure:
  - tests/ut/db/db_test.go
  - tests/ut/logger/logger_test.go
  - tests/ut/handler/{errors,health,response}_test.go
  - tests/ut/middleware/{error,logger,recovery,version}_test.go
  - tests/ut/domain/v{1..4}/handler_test.go
  - tests/ut/domain/v5/{handler,migrate,seed}_test.go
- Add tests/it/healthcheck_test.go (extracted from api_test.go)
- Export db.BuildDSN and add db.InitWithDialector for DI-friendly testing
- Update docs/architecture.md to reflect new test directory structure

commit.message:
feat(v5): add GORM + MySQL CRUD for articles resource

- Add gorm.io/gorm v1.31.1 and gorm.io/driver/mysql v1.6.0
- Add app/db/db.go: GORM singleton with env-based DSN (DB_HOST/PORT/USER/PASSWORD/NAME)
- Add app/domain/v5/model.go: Article GORM model with soft delete
- Add app/domain/v5/migrate.go: AutoMigrate for articles table
- Add app/domain/v5/seed.go: seed 3 sample articles (DB_SEED=true to enable)
- Add app/domain/v5/handler.go: full CRUD (list/create/get/update/delete)
  - listArticles: pagination with page/per_page query params
  - createArticle: JSON binding with required title validation
  - getArticleByID: 404 on missing record
  - updateArticle: partial update; reload from DB after save
  - deleteArticle: soft delete via GORM DeletedAt
- Update docker-compose.yml: add mysql:8.0 service with healthcheck and volume
- Update main.go: call db.Init(), v5.RunMigrations(), conditional v5.RunSeed()
- Update router.go: register v5 routes under /api/v5
- Add tests/ut/v5_handler_test.go: unit tests with go-sqlmock
- Add v5 integration tests to tests/it/api_test.go
- Add docs/api.md: full Swagger-style API reference for all endpoints
- Update docs/architecture.md: link to api.md, add v5/db package description
- Update README.md: add GORM/MySQL to tech stack, add DB env vars, link api.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add reusable PresetToolbar UI component

Add a toolbar component with Save, Load, and Copy-from-chart
functionality for any settings section. Uses vanilla DOM patterns
consistent with the rest of the codebase.

- PresetToolbar class with PresetToolbarOptions interface
- Save button triggers showInputDialog for preset naming
- Load/Copy <select> dropdowns with dynamic option refresh on focus
- Full accessibility: aria-labels on all controls
- 22 tests covering rendering, callbacks, reset, and edge cases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add LevelPresetStore with localStorage-backed level mapping presets

- LevelPresetStore follows MappingStore pattern (extends EventEmitter, IStorage DI)
- CRUD: getPresets(), getPreset(name), savePreset(), deletePreset()
- Validation: non-empty name, non-empty levelMappings, rawLevel + displayTitle required
- Preserves levelDisplayMode and managerDisplayTitle
- Handles corrupt localStorage gracefully
- 17 tests covering all behaviors (TDD: tests written first)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: arbol
repository.description: Browser-based org chart editor with real-time D3 visualization

commit.message:
feat: add CategoryPresetStore with localStorage-backed category presets

Implements CRUD for category presets following the MappingStore pattern:
- getPresets(), getPreset(name), savePreset(), deletePreset()
- Validation for name, categories array, and category fields
- EventEmitter for change notifications
- Graceful handling of corrupt localStorage
- 16 TDD tests covering all operations and edge cases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
